articles.summary: Google rankings are just one signal. Your brand is also being mentioned (or not) in ChatGPT answers, YouTube videos, Reddit threads, and TikTok.

articles.title: ↗️ See how your brand is performing
WHY DOES EVERYONE HATE AI?
articles.summary: Public hostility toward AI stems from a mix of distrust in tech, economic anxiety, and cultural resistance to synthetic tools. Public opinion had already turned against tech before AI launched due to issues like the Cambridge Analytica scandal and concerns about social media's effect on mental health. Many people fear job loss because AI arrived during a weak economic moment and threatens knowledge work rather than physical labor. Creatives shape culture and view AI as a threat to identity and artistic work, which fuels backlash online. Adoption will require companies to focus on real benefits like medical breakthroughs, highlight everyday users instead of executives, and support worker retraining as AI reshapes labor markets.

articles.title: AI IS MUCH BIGGER THAN YOU THINK
articles.summary: AI usage is far larger than commonly reported, with 45B monthly sessions worldwide and 5.4B in the US. Globally, AI now accounts for 56% of search traffic and 34% in the US, with 83% of activity occurring in mobile apps worldwide and 75% in the US. ChatGPT dominates the market, holding 89% of global AI sessions and 86% in the US, while search traffic continues to grow alongside AI, proving usage is additive rather than zero-sum. AI adoption is rapidly expanding, particularly for “asking” prompts, and the overall search ecosystem is growing by 26% worldwide since 2023. AI is creating a larger pie rather than replacing traditional search.

articles.title: HOW TO BUILD INSANELY CREATIVE DIGITAL PR CAMPAIGNS WITH KATY POWELL
articles.summary: Creative digital PR campaigns require bold ideas, strong validation, and clear measurement tied to business outcomes. Test ideas through topic analysis and journalist feedback before committing to them. Successful campaigns often include multiple story angles and realistic timelines of 4 to 6 weeks to reduce risk if the main concept fails. Research can also reveal strong campaign hooks, such as a study that found 70% of people who gamble do not research their bets. Performance measurement should go beyond backlinks and include brand mentions, social reach, and search visibility to connect campaign results to real marketing goals.

articles.title: THE SIMPLE GENIUS BEHIND THIS LONG-FORGOTTEN GOOGLE CHROME AD
articles.summary: Committing to one clear strength builds more credibility than competing on range. Research on the goal dilution effect shows that adding benefits to a product claim actively weakens persuasion, with a study demonstrating a 12% drop in perceived effectiveness when a second benefit was listed alongside the first. Chrome ignored its security features and Gmail integration at launch and led only with speed. The browser now holds 71% of the global market. Five Guys followed the same logic in 1986, limiting its menu to burgers and fries and growing over 700% in six years.

articles.title: AI-GENERATED CONTENT VS. AI-ACCELERATED CONTENT
articles.summary: A CEO grew LinkedIn reach to 100-300K impressions per post by using Claude to analyze hundreds of high performing posts from his account and industry creators. The AI identified patterns in structure and storytelling to create a repeatable content formula. It also analyzed comments on top posts to surface recurring audience questions and themes. The CEO records ideas with WhisprFlow, drops them into Claude, and the AI shapes them into the framework for quick editing and publishing.

articles.title: NEW: NON-BRANDED QUERIES FILTER IN GSC
articles.summary: Google Search Console has released a "Non-branded queries" filter, allowing you to remove non-branded queries from your data. Navigate to Performance > Search Results > Add Filter > Query to access it. The filter only applies to data back to late February.

articles.title: 5 DESIGN SKILLS TO SHARPEN IN THE AI ERA
articles.summary: Visual craft ranks first with 58% of designers and hiring managers citing it as the top skill, while systems thinking, cross-functional collaboration, and AI fluency follow closely behind. The underlying tension the Figma report surfaces is that AI can accelerate exploration and prototype generation, but it cannot challenge assumptions, follow unexpected creative directions, or decide when something technically works but feels wrong.

articles.title: WHAT SHOULD YOUR COMPANY'S AI SOUND LIKE TO CUSTOMERS?
articles.summary: As AI voice systems move toward handling 70-80% of customer service interactions by 2028-2029, most organizations have no framework for calibrating how confident their AI sounds relative to what it actually knows. Research in vocal psychology shows confident delivery increases persuasiveness and triggers authority deference independent of information quality, meaning a moderately certain AI recommendation can be heard as definitive when spoken aloud. The fix requires institutional governance that classifies interactions by risk and assigns vocal delivery rules accordingly, paired with real-time systems that adjust tone based on model confidence and user context.

articles.title: SEO TIP: CLEAN UP YOUR SITE NAVIGATION
articles.summary: Clean up cluttered navigation menus to avoid spreading authority and traffic too thin across the site, which confuses people and Google.

articles.title: 22 RETENTION FLOW TIPS
articles.summary: Retention improves when brands capture leads early, remove buying friction, and push for repeat purchases quickly.
